Why: Smooth, straight bore → minimal turbulence, lowest pressure drop, best flow rate. Ideal for tubing or water block channels.
Core tube is reasonably smooth + massive external surface area from fins/grid. Great for a radiator section or block exterior, but more restrictive than Pride internally.
Has a solid core tube, but the helical cage + rings add complexity/possible weak points for sealing. Extra surface area is nice but flow is compromised.
Concentric flared/twisted shells will create turbulence and dead zones. Hard to print/seal cleanly.
Corrugated walls create constant turbulence and higher friction → significantly worse flow.
Chaotic waving path = very long effective length + sharp direction changes → high pressure drop and turbulence.
Hourglass constrictions repeatedly narrow the bore → massive flow restriction and pressure spikes. Pump would hate this.
